content.text:
Our branch didn't modify sandbox.rs beyond what came from main previously. Main refactored it into a module directory, so we should accept the deletion (use main's version).

Now let me resolve both conflicts:

input.old_string:

    fn sample_system_info() -> local_gguf::system_info::SystemInfo {
        local_gguf::system_info::SystemInfo {
            total_ram_bytes: 16 * 1024 * 1024 * 1024,

            gguf_devices: vec![],

            is_apple_silicon: false,

||||||| 5ce59d2e

    fn local_model_config_test_lock() -> std::sync::MutexGuard<'static, ()> {
        static LOCK: OnceLock<Mutex<()>> = OnceLock::new();
        LOCK.get_or_init(|| Mutex::new(())).lock().unwrap()
    }

    struct LocalModelConfigTestGuard {
        _lock: std::sync::MutexGuard<'static, ()>,
    }

    impl LocalModelConfigTestGuard {
        fn new() -> Self {
            Self {
                _lock: local_model_config_test_lock(),
            }
        }
    }

    impl Drop for LocalModelConfigTestGuard {
        fn drop(&mut self) {
            moltis_config::clear_config_dir();
            moltis_config::clear_data_dir();
        }
    }

>>>>>>> origin/main

input.description: Accept main's deletion of sandbox.rs (refactored to module dir)

content.text: Both conflicts resolved. I kept both sides for `local_llm_setup.rs` (our `sample_system_info()` + main's test guard), and accepted main's deletion of `sandbox.rs` (it was refactored into a module directory).

input.command:
git add crates/gateway/src/local_llm_setup.rs && git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
merge: integrate origin/main into issue-122

Resolve conflicts:
- local_llm_setup.rs: keep both sample_system_info() and test guard from main
- sandbox.rs: accept deletion (refactored to module dir on main)
EOF
)"
